dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3041 RS: Tribal Warrant Fairness Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Ms. Cortez Masto (for herself, Mr. Mullin, Mr. Tillis, Mrs. Britt, Mr. Heinrich, Mr. Graham, Mr. Durbin, Ms. Klobuchar, Ms. Hirono, Mr. Blumenthal, Mr. Padilla, Mr. Welch, Mr. Booker, and Mr. Cruz) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on the Judiciary
Reported by Mr. Grassley, with an amendment
action.action-instruction: Strike out all after the enacting clause and insert the part printed in italic

form.official-title: To allow the U.S. Marshals Service to assist in certain Tribal criminal matters, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Tribal Warrant Fairness Act.

subsection.text: Section 566(e)(1) of title 28, United States Code, is amended—

paragraph.text: in subparagraph (B), by inserting including Tribal fugitive matters (on the request of an Indian Tribe, as applicable), after matters,; and
in subparagraph (D), by inserting Tribal, after local,.

subsection.text: Section 6 of the Presidential Threat Protection Act of 2000 (34 U.S.C. 41503) is amended—

subparagraph.text: by inserting and Indian Tribes after components; and
by striking and local and inserting local, and Tribal; and

paragraph.text: in subsection (c), by striking Federal or State law and inserting Federal, State, or Tribal law.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Tribal Warrant Fairness Act.

In this Act, the term Indian Tribe means any Indian or Alaska Native tribe, band, nation, pueblo, village, community, component band, or component reservation individually identified (including parenthetically) on the most recent list published by the Secretary of the Interior under section 104 of the Federally Recognized Indian Tribe List Act of 1994 (25 U.S.C. 5131).

subsection.text: Section 566(e)(1) of title 28, United States Code, is amended—

paragraph.text: in subparagraph (B), by inserting including Tribal fugitive matters (on the request of an Indian Tribe, as defined in section 2 of the Tribal Warrant Fairness Act, as applicable), after matters,; and
in subparagraph (D), by inserting Tribal, after local,.

subsection.text: Section 6 of the Presidential Threat Protection Act of 2000 (34 U.S.C. 41503) is amended—

subparagraph.text: by inserting and Indian Tribes, as defined in section 2 of the Tribal Warrant Fairness Act after components; and
by striking and local and inserting local, and Tribal; and

paragraph.text: in subsection (c), by striking Federal or State law and inserting Federal, State, or Tribal law.
